parameters.title: Parameters
parameters.description: Parameters for controlling execution of the pipeline.

run.description: Selectively run tests as comma-delimited values.
run.help_text: Tests to run as comma-delimited values. E.g. --run TEST_SUCCESS,TEST_INPUT. Case insensitive. Note this excludes all other tests.

skip.description: Tests to skip as comma-delimited values.
skip.help_text: Tests to skip as comma-delimited values. E.g. --skip TEST_SUCCESS,TEST_INPUT. Case insensitive.

remoteFile.description: Path to remote file to download and use.
remoteFile.help_text: Path to a remote file to use within the pipeline. This mimics a remote set of files such as reference data that may need to be retrieved prior to analysis. By default this is not specified and the test is not ran, add a remote file using standard Nextflow filenaming to pull a file from your storage (e.g. an S3 bucket or shared storage).

container.help_text: Specifies the container URI. By default, this is an Ubuntu container on quay.io with no usage limits.

gpu.description: Whether to test GPU utilization within a process.

outdir.description: Path to use for publishing output files.
outdir.help_text: Path to a remote folder to publish files to. If not specified, this is a subfolder within the working directory but if specified it will publish files to the path specified in this parameter.

fusion_options.title: Fusion Filesystem Options
fusion_options.description: Configure Fusion filesystem validation and diagnostics.

fusion.description: Run Fusion filesystem diagnostics via 'fusion doctor'.
fusion.help_text: When enabled, run the TEST_FUSION_DOCTOR process to validate Fusion requirements.

fusion_redact.description: Redact PII from the Fusion diagnostics report.
fusion_redact.help_text: When enabled, instructs 'fusion doctor' to mask personally identifiable information (hostnames, IPs, bucket names, etc.) from the diagnostic output.

fusion_kernel_version_min.description: Minimum Linux kernel version(s) required for Fusion.
fusion_kernel_version_min.help_text: Comma-separated list of kernel version thresholds to sweep. FUSION_DOCTOR runs once per value. E.g. '5.10' for a single check, or '5.10,5.15,6.1' to sweep multiple thresholds. AWS Forge images ship 5.10+ (AL2) or 6.1+ (AL2023); GCP and Azure ship 5.15+.

fusion_memory_gb_min.description: Minimum memory (GB) required for Fusion, as a comma-separated sweep.
fusion_memory_gb_min.help_text: Comma-separated list of memory thresholds (in GB) to sweep. FUSION_DOCTOR runs once per value. E.g. '8' for a single check, or '4,8,16' to sweep multiple thresholds.

fusion_disk_gb_min.description: Minimum local disk size (GB) required for Fusion, as a comma-separated sweep.
fusion_disk_gb_min.help_text: Comma-separated list of disk size thresholds (in GB) to sweep. FUSION_DOCTOR runs once per value. E.g. '200' for a single check, or '100,200,950' to sweep multiple thresholds. Seqera docs require at least 200 GB local temp storage; 400 GB+ for files >100 GB. Cloud-specific profiles override this with values appropriate to that cloud's disk increments.

fusion_nvme_required.description: Whether NVMe local storage is required, as a comma-separated sweep.
fusion_nvme_required.help_text: Comma-separated list of boolean values to sweep ('true', 'false', or 'false,true'). FUSION_DOCTOR runs once per value. Fusion performs best on NVMe-backed instance storage. Set to 'true' when using NVMe instance families (e.g. AWS m6id, GCP local SSD, Azure 'd'-suffix VMs).

fusion_cpu_cores_min.description: Minimum CPU cores required for Fusion, as a comma-separated sweep.
fusion_cpu_cores_min.help_text: Comma-separated list of CPU core thresholds to sweep. FUSION_DOCTOR runs once per value. E.g. '4' for a single check, or '2,4,16' to sweep multiple thresholds. Seqera recommends 16 vCPUs for large production pipelines.

fusion_open_files_min.description: Minimum open file descriptor limit required for Fusion, as a comma-separated sweep.
fusion_open_files_min.help_text: Comma-separated list of open file descriptor thresholds to sweep. FUSION_DOCTOR runs once per value. Fusion opens many file handles for FUSE operations and cloud storage connections. E.g. '65535' (Linux default), '131072' (recommended), or '65535,131072,1048576' to sweep all tiers.

fusion_cache_path.description: Filesystem path(s) for Fusion cache directory, as a comma-separated sweep.
fusion_cache_path.help_text: Comma-separated list of cache directory paths to sweep. FUSION_DOCTOR runs once per value. Used to verify sufficient disk space is available at that location. E.g. '/tmp' or '/tmp,/scratch'.

fusion_read_only_buckets.description: Comma-separated list of read-only cloud storage buckets to validate access.
fusion_read_only_buckets.help_text: Comma-separated URIs of cloud storage buckets that require read-only access. The diagnostics process will validate if the task can read from these buckets. Supports AWS S3 (s3://), Google Cloud Storage (gs://), and Azure Blob Storage (az://). Example: 's3://reference-data,gs://shared-files'

fusion_read_write_buckets.description: Comma-separated list of read-write cloud storage buckets to validate access.
fusion_read_write_buckets.help_text: Comma-separated URIs of cloud storage buckets that require read-write access. The diagnostics process will validate if the task can read from and write to these buckets. Supports AWS S3 (s3://), Google Cloud Storage (gs://), and Azure Blob Storage (az://). Example: 's3://results,gs://output-data'
